title: Fix Admin Dashboard redirect after login in production

requirements.summary: Fix admin login flow to reliably redirect to Admin Dashboard after successful authentication in production, preventing the 'Go to Admin Setup or Return to Home' fallback screen from appearing for authenticated admin users
acceptanceCriteria: After successful admin login in production, the user is navigated directly to the Admin Dashboard page.
The 'Go to Admin Setup or Return to Home' fallback is not shown to already-authenticated admin users.
The redirect behavior is identical between draft and production environments.
AdminGuard correctly recognizes the authenticated admin principal/session in production and does not redirect them to setup.
The admin login page clears any stale auth state before checking admin existence to avoid race conditions.

file_operations.description: Fix post-login navigation to use programmatic redirect (router.navigate) to '/admin/dashboard' after all authentication methods succeed. Ensure navigation fires only after actor and identity state are fully settled. Add query cache invalidation for 'isAdmin' and 'adminExists' queries immediately after successful login to prevent stale cache from causing false negatives. Wait for actor to be ready before attempting navigation. Remove any premature navigation that occurs before async state updates complete.

requirements.summary: Fix AdminGuard to properly wait for actor and identity initialization before evaluating admin status, showing a loading state instead of prematurely rendering the 'admin setup' screen in production
acceptanceCriteria: AdminGuard displays a loading indicator while actor or identity is being initialized.
AdminGuard does not render the 'admin setup' or 'access denied' UI until the identity and admin status check are fully resolved.
In production, authenticated admin users are granted access to the Admin Dashboard without seeing the setup screen.

file_operations.description: Fix AdminGuard to prevent premature evaluation of admin status before actor/identity initialization completes. Add explicit checks for actorFetching state from useActor hook. Show loading UI when actor is fetching, identity is being loaded, or admin status query is in flight. Only evaluate adminExists and isAdmin after both actor and identity are confirmed ready. Ensure the guard does not render 'admin setup' or 'access denied' UI until all async dependencies are resolved. Add proper loading state tracking that combines actor readiness, identity readiness, and query states.

requirements.summary: Ensure AdminLoginPage uses reliable post-login navigation that fires after actor/identity state is fully settled, preventing race conditions with query cache
acceptanceCriteria: After login succeeds via any method (email/password, biometric, Internet Identity), navigation to Admin Dashboard fires reliably in production.
Navigation does not occur before the authentication state and actor are ready.
No stale query cache causes the admin check to return a false negative immediately after login.

file_operations.description: Add await for queryClient.invalidateQueries targeting both 'isAdmin' and 'adminExists' query keys immediately after successful authentication in all login methods (email/password, biometric, Internet Identity). Introduce a small delay (e.g., 100-200ms) after invalidation to allow React Query to refetch before navigation. Use router.navigate('/admin/dashboard') with replace option to prevent back-button navigation to login page. Ensure all authentication success handlers follow the same pattern: authenticate → invalidate queries → wait briefly → navigate.
